title: Referential Integrity Management on MySQL
text: Referential Integrity Management on MySQL Referential integrity is handled by Uniface. Commit and Rollback The commit and rollback instructions are fully supported by MySQL Server, provided you use the correct storage engine. Two-phase commit is not supported. Locking All Uniface locking types are supported. The default locking type is cautious. You can override cautious locking for individual entities by specifying optimistic locking in the entity definition in the application model. read/lock You can specify paranoid locking by using the read /lock ProcScript command in the read trigger of an entity. U_VERSION The MySQL connector supports the U_VERSION mechanism to improve locking performance. It is strongly recommended that you use U_VERSION, because this significantly reduces the chance of lock conflicts. Transactions Transactions are started implicitly by MySQL when required. Related Links Transaction Control A transaction is a complete, logical unit of work. It usually involves adding, deleting or updating one or more logically related records in one or more databases. With Uniface, you can explicitly define a transaction with the commit ProcScript instruction. If an error occurs, a transaction can be rolled back with the rollback ProcScript instruction. U_VERSION for Improving Locking Performance Uniface supports a version mechanism which can improve performance by using a field called U_VERSION. U_VERSION is a one-byte field which you must assign to an entity for it to work. In other words, it is not automatically available. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
